verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine that exists predominantly in a latent form (LAP-TGF-β1 complex). It reaches the outer face of the plasma membrane specifically on activated Tregs, platelets, and stimulated B cells via covalent (disulfide) linkage to GARP/LRRC32, a transmembrane protein. This GARP:latent-TGF-β1 complex is stably surface-displayed and targeted by therapeutic mAbs. LRRC33 similarly anchors it on myeloid/microglial cells. The surface presentation is cell-state- and cell-type-restricted (activation-dependent on Tregs/B cells; constitutive on platelets), and the disulfide bond to GARP meets the wash-resistance criterion. The dominant pool is soluble/ECM-deposited, making this contextual rather than yes.
